=== runner shell started at 2026-04-17T08:17:26.000Z ===
Script path: /c/Users/me/work/ai/promptbook/prompts/2026-04-6410-agents-server-chat-optimistic-profile-to-chat-message.sh

--- raw input ---
cd "/c/Users/me/work/ai/promptbook"

copilot -p "$(cat <<'GITHUB_COPILOT_PROMPT'

Optimistic navigation from agent profile to agent chat with immediate user message

-   The agent chat page should appear immediately after the user triggers navigation from the agent profile page (e.g., by pressing a quick button or submitting a first message), without waiting for server confirmation.
-   The typed user message must be rendered in the chat immediately, matching the “new chat + send message” behavior (optimistic message bubble), while the server request/processing continues in the background.
-   The background processing must reconcile with the server response: replace or update the optimistic message (status, temp id, streaming state) and then render the assistant response when it arrives.
-   The UI must handle navigation + streaming consistently (no duplicated messages, correct ordering, correct auto-scroll behavior).
-   If the server ultimately rejects the message or the request fails, update the optimistic message to an error state (and optionally allow retry), without breaking the already-visible chat.

## How to Contribute

-   **Add changes in [Changelog](/changelog/_current-preversion.md)**
-   **Test your changes** to ensure they work as expected, use `npm test`

## Code Style

-   Follow the existing code style and structure.
-   Use comments to explain complex logic.

## Emoji in `[brackets]`

-   `[any emoji]` Connects multiple places that are related to each other across the repository
-   `[number]` Connects multiple places that are related to each other across the file
-   `[🧠]` Marks a place where there is something to decide and think about.
-   `[🕕]` List of models _(that should be progresively updated)_
    -   Prompt: Update available models and their prices, search online
-   `[🔼]` Marks an entity (function, class, type,...) in other project (like Promptbook.studio) which should be moved to this repository
-   `[🚉]` Marks an types / interfaces / structures fully serializable as JSON, not marking `string_` and `number_` prefixed aliases
-   `[🧹]` Need to implement garbage collection
-   `[🐣]` Easter eggs
-   `[💩]` Shitty code that needs refactoring
-   `$` When entity (function, class) starts by `$`, it means it is not pure and can have side effects.
-   3x `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed before releasing a pre-release version.
-   4x (and more) `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed as soon as possible.
-   `@@@` Marks a place where text / documentation / ... must be written.
-   [⚫] Code in this file should never be published in any package
-   [🟢] Code in this file should never be published into packages that could be imported into browser environment
-   [🔵] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/browser`
-   [🟡] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/cli`
-   [💞] Ignore a discrepancy between file name and entity name

## Generated code

Across the repository, there are several places where code has been generated automatically.
Do not edit these places manually, as they will be overwritten by the code generation process. Edit the source files instead.

> ⚠️ WARNING: This code has been generated so that any manual changes will be overwritten

## Dictionary

-   **Promptbook Engine vs. Agent Server** sre two distinct parts you should know the difference:
    -   **Promptbook Engine** is the core engine that powers AI agents, it is located in `/src` folder. It is framework-agnostic and can be used in different applications. It can run as standalone javascript/typescript library, CLI environment, or be integrated into different applications. It can be also runned in Agent Server:
    -   **Agent Server** is a specific application that uses the Promptbook Engine to provide a web and API interface for creating and interacting with AI agents. It is located in `/apps/agents-server` folder.
-   **Commitments** are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents
    -   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, etc.
    -   Commitments are in the folder `/src/commitments`
    -   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g., `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, etc. on a begining of the line and end by new co
    -   Agent source with commitments is parsed by two functions:
        -   `parseAgentSource` which is a lightweight parser for agent source, it parses basic information and its purpose is to be quick and synchronous. The commitments there are hardcoded.
        -   `createAgentModelRequirements` which is an asynchronous function that creates model requirements it applies each commitment one by one and works asynchronously.

## Common rules

-   Always analyze the context and requirements before generating any code.
-   Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code.
-   Write JSDoc comments for all entities - functions, classes, types, top-level constants, etc.
    -   When this entity is exported from the file and it is under `src` folder *(not for example in the `apps` folder)*, it must be marked either as `@public` or `@private` at the end of the JSDoc comment.
    -   For example: "@private internal utility of <Chat/>" / "@public exported from `@promptbook/browser`"
    -   If you don't know, prefer to mark it as private, we can always change it to public later, but changing from public to private may cause breaking changes.
-   After code change, run the following tests to ensure everything works as expected:
    1) `npm run test-name-discrepancies` - Tests that file names match the exported names.
    2) `npm run test-spellcheck` - When using some new word, add it into the [dictionary](other/cspell-dictionaries).
    3) `npm run test-lint` - Linting.
    4) `npm run test-types` - Checks TypeScript types.
    5) `npm run test-package-generation` - Tests build/package generation and public/private JSDoc coverage.
    6) `npm run test-unit` - Unit tests.
    7) `npm run test-app-agents-server` - Tests that the Agents Server app is working correctly.
-   You don't need to run every test, run them only when you make changes which may cause them to fail.
-   You (the AI coding agent) are running inside a Node process, so do not kill all Node processes such as `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`. If you need to stop something you spawned, kill only that specific process, for example by PID or by port.

## Additional context

-   Attached images (if any) are relative to the root of the project.

### The Agents Server menu *(as additional context)*

The menu of the agent server looks like this:

1. The navigation hierarchy
    - Icon and Server name _(for example Promptbook Agents Server)_
    - arrow ">" and Agents or picked agent name (organized in folders)
    - arrow ">" and the view Profile / Chat / Book of the agent or nothing if no agent is picked
2. The menu items
    - Documentation
    - System
3. Control panel and user menu
    - Control panel
    - User menu with the avatar and the name of the user

### Database migrations for Agents server *(as additional context)*

-   Migrations are located in `apps/agents-server/src/database/migrations`
-   Be aware that table names in migrations have prefix `prefix_` _(look at existing migrations for reference)_
-   Migrations should be backwards compatible:
    -   Meaning that earlier versions of the server should be able to run with the database after migration without any issues.
    -   This is important because we want to have the same database for production and preview environments.
    -   This database will be migrated to the latest version of the preview environment, but the production environment should be able to run with it without any issues.
    -   The only thing that can happen is that older versions of the server will not be able to use new features.
    -   Adding new columns, tables, etc. is fine, but do not remove or rename existing ones, and do not change the meaning of existing columns or tables.
    -   When in doubt, prefer to add new things instead of changing existing ones.

### Metadata of Agents server *(as additional context)*

-   There is a table called `Metadata`.
-   It has `key` and `value` fields.
-   It is a similar concept to configuration, but this configuration can be changed by administrators in the Agents Server website.

### Book Language blueprint *(as additional context)*

Book language is a domain-specific language used for defining AI agents in the Promptbook Engine and Agents server.
It has lightweight syntax and keywords (the commitments) that allow you to define the "soul" of the agent.
The book language is designed to be human-readable and easy to write, while also being powerful enough to express complex agent behaviors.

Every agent has its source defined in the book language, which is called "agent source". The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.
This agent source is internally converted to a structured format called "agent model requirements" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.

There is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.
Use it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.

#### Commitments *(as additional context and part of Book Language)*

Commitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.

-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.
-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.
-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.
-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:
    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`
    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`
-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:
    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`

## Coding rules

-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.
-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.
-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.
-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.
-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.
    -   Format errors as markdown, for example `variables` should be in backticks and important notes can be in bold.
-   Constants should always be `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`.
-   Boolean variables should always be prefixed with `is`, for example `isUserChatJobLeaseExpired` or `IS_DEBUG_MODE`.
-   Do not use abbreviations, for example use `isExpired` instead of `isExp`, `translateMessage` instead of `t`, etc.
    -   It is fine to use well-known abbreviations, for example `id`, `url`, `html`, etc.
-   When writing multiline strings, use `spaceTrim` utility.
-   Do only the change described in the prompt. Do not add any additional features or make any additional changes that are not described there.
    -   If you find some critical issue that is not described in the prompt, report it to `./AGENT_REPORT.md` in the root of the project.

It can be also runned in Agent Server:\n    -   **Agent Server** is a specific application that uses the Promptbook Engine to provide a web and API interface for creating and interacting with AI agents. It is located in `/apps/agents-server` folder.\n-   **Commitments** are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents\n    -   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, etc.\n    -   Commitments are in the folder `/src/commitments`\n    -   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g., `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, etc. on a begining of the line and end by new co\n    -   Agent source with commitments is parsed by two functions:\n        -   `parseAgentSource` which is a lightweight parser for agent source, it parses basic information and its purpose is to be quick and synchronous. The commitments there are hardcoded.\n        -   `createAgentModelRequirements` which is an asynchronous function that creates model requirements it applies each commitment one by one and works asynchronously.\n\n## Common rules\n\n-   Always analyze the context and requirements before generating any code.\n-   Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code.\n-   Write JSDoc comments for all entities - functions, classes, types, top-level constants, etc.\n    -   When this entity is exported from the file and it is under `src` folder *(not for example in the `apps` folder)*, it must be marked either as `@public` or `@private` at the end of the JSDoc comment.\n    -   For example: \"@private internal utility of <Chat/>\" / \"@public exported from `@promptbook/browser`\"\n    -   If you don't know, prefer to mark it as private, we can always change it to public later, but changing from public to private may cause breaking changes.\n-   After code change, run the following tests to ensure everything works as expected:\n    1) `npm run test-name-discrepancies` - Tests that file names match the exported names.\n    2) `npm run test-spellcheck` - When using some new word, add it into the [dictionary](other/cspell-dictionaries).\n    3) `npm run test-lint` - Linting.\n    4) `npm run test-types` - Checks TypeScript types.\n    5) `npm run test-package-generation` - Tests build/package generation and public/private JSDoc coverage.\n    6) `npm run test-unit` - Unit tests.\n    7) `npm run test-app-agents-server` - Tests that the Agents Server app is working correctly.\n-   You don't need to run every test, run them only when you make changes which may cause them to fail.\n-   You (the AI coding agent) are running inside a Node process, so do not kill all Node processes such as `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`. If you need to stop something you spawned, kill only that specific process, for example by PID or by port.\n\n## Additional context\n\n-   Attached images (if any) are relative to the root of the project.\n\n### The Agents Server menu *(as additional context)*\n\nThe menu of the agent server looks like this:\n\n1. The navigation hierarchy\n    - Icon and Server name _(for example Promptbook Agents Server)_\n    - arrow \">\" and Agents or picked agent name (organized in folders)\n    - arrow \">\" and the view Profile / Chat / Book of the agent or nothing if no agent is picked\n2. The menu items\n    - Documentation\n    - System\n3. Control panel and user menu\n    - Control panel\n    - User menu with the avatar and the name of the user\n\n### Database migrations for Agents server *(as additional context)*\n\n-   Migrations are located in `apps/agents-server/src/database/migrations`\n-   Be aware that table names in migrations have prefix `prefix_` _(look at existing migrations for reference)_\n-   Migrations should be backwards compatible:\n    -   Meaning that earlier versions of the server should be able to run with the database after migration without any issues.\n    -   This is important because we want to have the same database for production and preview environments.\n    -   This database will be migrated to the latest version of the preview environment, but the production environment should be able to run with it without any issues.\n    -   The only thing that can happen is that older versions of the server will not be able to use new features.\n    -   Adding new columns, tables, etc. is fine, but do not remove or rename existing ones, and do not change the meaning of existing columns or tables.\n    -   When in doubt, prefer to add new things instead of changing existing ones.\n\n### Metadata of Agents server *(as additional context)*\n\n-   There is a table called `Metadata`.\n-   It has `key` and `value` fields.\n-   It is a similar concept to configuration, but this configuration can be changed by administrators in the Agents Server website.\n\n### Book Language blueprint *(as additional context)*\n\nBook language is a domain-specific language used for defining AI agents in the Promptbook Engine and Agents server.\nIt has lightweight syntax and keywords (the commitments) that allow you to define the \"soul\" of the agent.\nThe book language is designed to be human-readable and easy to write, while also being powerful enough to express complex agent behaviors.\n\nEvery agent has its source defined in the book language, which is called \"agent source\". The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.\nThis agent source is internally converted to a structured format called \"agent model requirements\" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.\n\nThere is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.\nUse it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.\n\n#### Commitments *(as additional context and part of Book Language)*\n\nCommitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.\n\n-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.\n-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.\n-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.\n-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:\n    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`\n    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`\n-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:\n    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`\n\n## Coding rules\n\n-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.\n-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.\n-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.\n-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.\n-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.\n    -   Format errors as markdown, for example `variables` should be in backticks and important notes can be in bold.\n-   Constants should always be `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`.\n-   Boolean variables should always be prefixed with `is`, for example `isUserChatJobLeaseExpired` or `IS_DEBUG_MODE`.\n-   Do not use abbreviations, for example use `isExpired` instead of `isExp`, `translateMessage` instead of `t`, etc.\n    -   It is fine to use well-known abbreviations, for example `id`, `url`, `html`, etc.\n-   When writing multiline strings, use `spaceTrim` utility.\n-   Do only the change described in the prompt. UseAgentProfileChatNavigationProps,\n62. ): UseAgentProfileChatNavigationResult {\n63.     const { chatRoute, isHistoryEnabled } = props;\n64.     const router = useRouter();\n65.     const [isNavigatingToChat, setIsNavigatingToChat] = useState(false);\n66.     const pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);\n67.     const    }, [chatRoute, router]);\n72. \n73.     /**\n74.      * Clears any pending hard-navigation fallback timer.\n75.      */\n76.     const clearPendingNavigationFallback = useCallback(() => {\n77.         if (pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef.current === null) {\n78.             return;\n79.         }\n80. \n81.         clearTimeout(pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef.current);\n82.         pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef.current = null;\n83.     }, []);\n84. \n85.     useEffect(() => {\n86.         return () => {\n87.             clearPendingNavigationFallback();\n88. \n89. transitioning to the full chat page and schedules\n98.      * a safety reset if the navigation stalls or is aborted.\n99.      */\n100.     const startNavigatingToChat = useCallback(() => {\n101.         setIsNavigatingToChat(true);\n102. \n103.         if (isNavigatingToChatResetTimeoutRef.current !== null) {\n104.             clearTimeout(isNavigatingToChatResetTimeoutRef.current);\n105.         }\n106. \n107.         isNavigatingToChatResetTimeoutRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n108.             isNavigatingToChatResetTimeoutRef.current = null;\n109.             console.warn(\n110.                 '[AgentProfileChat] Navigation to chat stalled — resetting transitioning state so the page remains interactive',\n111.             );\n112.             setIsNavigatingToChat(false);\n113.         }, PROFILE_CHAT_NAVIGATION_STATE_RESET_MS);\n114.     }, []);\n115. \n116.     /**\n117.      * Navigates to one chat destination and falls back to hard navigation when\n118.      * the SPA transition does not change the current location in time.\n119.      */\n120.     const navigateToDestination = useCallback(\n121.         (destination: string) => {\n122.             startNavigatingToChat();\n123.             dispatchNavigationProgressStart({ href: destination, source: 'router' });\n124.             router.push(destination);\n125. \n126.             clearPendingNavigationFallback();\n127. \n128.             const locationBeforePush = `${window.location.pathname}${window.location.search}${window.location.hash}`;\n129.             const normalizedDestination = normalizeDestinationForLocationComparison(destination);\n130.             pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n131.                 pendingNavigationFallbackTimeoutRef.current = null;\n132.                 const locationAfterPush = `${window.location.pathname}${window.location.search}${window.location.hash}`;\n133.                 if (locationAfterPush === locationBeforePush && locationAfterPush !== normalizedDestination) {\n134.                     console.warn('[AgentProfileChat] SPA navigation stalled — falling back to hard navigation', {\n135.                         destination,\n136.                     });\n137.                     window.location.assign(destination);\n138.                 }\n139.             }, PROFILE_CHAT_NAVIGATION_FALLBACK_DELAY_MS);\n140. \n141.             return Promise.resolve();\n142.         },\n143. ({ shouldForceNewChat }: { shouldForceNewChat: boolean }) => {\n148.             const destination = buildAgentChatDestinationUrl(chatRoute, { shouldForceNewChat, isHistoryEnabled });\n149.             return navigateToDestination(destination);\n150.         },\n151.  return pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.get(chatId) || EMPTY_PENDING_OUTBOUND_MESSAGES;\n }\n \n /**\n  * Broadcasts a store mutation to all optimistic outbound-message subscribers.\n  */\n function emitPendingOutboundMessagesStoreChange(): void {\n     for (const listener of pendingOutboundMessagesListeners) {\n         listener();\n     }\n }\n \n /**\n  * Returns a stable optimistic temporary message id for one client-generated\n  * outbound-message idempotency key.\n  *\n  * @param clientMessageId - Client-generated idempotency key.\n  * @returns Deterministic optimistic temporary message id.\n  */\n function createPendingOutboundMessageTempId(clientMessageId: string): string {\n     return `${PENDING_OUTBOUND_MESSAGE_TEMP_ID_PREFIX}:${clientMessageId}`;\n }\n \n /**\n  * Writes optimistic outbound messages for one chat and only emits when the\n  * snapshot meaningfully changed.\n  *\n  * @param chatId - Durable chat identifier.\n  * @param nextPendingOutboundMessages - Next optimistic messages for this chat.\n  */\n function setPendingOutboundMessagesForChat(\n     chatId: string,\n     nextPendingOutboundMessages: Array<PendingOutboundMessageRecord>,\n ): void {\n     const previousPendingOutboundMessages = pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.get(chatId) || EMPTY_PENDING_OUTBOUND_MESSAGES;\n \n     if (arePendingOutboundMessageSnapshotsEquivalent(previousPendingOutboundMessages, nextPendingOutboundMessages)) {\n         return;\n     }\n \n     if (nextPendingOutboundMessages.length === 0) {\n         pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.delete(chatId);\n     } else {\n         pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.set(chatId, nextPendingOutboundMessages);\n     }\n \n     emitPendingOutboundMessagesStoreChange();\n }\n \n /**\n  * Returns true when two optimistic outbound-message snapshots are equivalent.\n  *\n  * @param left - Previous optimistic snapshot.\n  * @param right - Next optimistic snapshot.\n  * @returns `true` when no subscriber-visible change occurred.\n  */\n function arePendingOutboundMessageSnapshotsEquivalent(\n     left: ReadonlyArray<PendingOutboundMessageRecord>,\n     right: ReadonlyArray<PendingOutboundMessageRecord>,\n ): boolean {\n     if (left === right) {\n         return true;\n     }\n \n     if (left.length !== right.length) {\n         return false;\n     }\n \n     for (let index = 0; index < left.length; index++) {\n         const leftMessage = left[index]!;\n         const rightMessage = right[index]!;\n \n         if (\n             leftMessage.tempId !== rightMessage.tempId ||\n             leftMessage.chatId !== rightMessage.chatId ||\n             leftMessage.clientMessageId !== rightMessage.clientMessageId ||\n             leftMessage.content !== rightMessage.content ||\n             leftMessage.createdAt !== rightMessage.createdAt ||\n             leftMessage.status !== rightMessage.status ||\n             leftMessage.errorMessage !== rightMessage.errorMessage ||\n             !areAttachmentsEquivalent(leftMessage.attachments, rightMessage.attachments) ||\n             serializeReplyingToSignature(leftMessage.replyingTo) !== serializeReplyingToSignature(rightMessage.replyingTo)\n         ) {\n             return false;\n         }\n     }\n \n     return true;\n }\n \n /**\n  * Returns true when two optimistic attachment collections are equivalent.\n  *\n  * @param left - Previous attachments snapshot.\n  * @param right - Next attachments snapshot.\n  * @returns `true` when both snapshots serialize equally.\n  */\n function areAttachmentsEquivalent(left: ChatMessage['attachments'], right: ChatMessage['attachments']): boolean {\n     if (!left?.length && !right?.length) {\n         return true;\n     }\n \n     return JSON.stringify(left || []) === JSON.stringify(right || []);\n }\n \n /**\n  * Returns the optimistic outbound messages for one selected chat and keeps the\n  * component subscribed to future mutations.\n  *\n  * @param chatId - Durable chat identifier or `null` when no chat is selected.\n  * @returns Optimistic outbound messages for the active chat.\n  */\n export function usePendingOutboundMessages(chatId: string | null): ReadonlyArray<PendingOutboundMessageRecord> {\n     return useSyncExternalStore(\n         subscribeToPendingOutboundMessages,\n         () => getPendingOutboundMessagesSnapshot(chatId),\n         () => EMPTY_PENDING_OUTBOUND_MESSAGES,\n     );\n }\n \n /**\n  * Queues or refreshes one optimistic outbound message for the active durable chat.\n  *\n  * Re-queueing an existing message keeps the same temporary id so the optimistic\n  * bubble remains stable across retries and options: QueuePendingOutboundMessageOptions,\n ): PendingOutboundMessageRecord {\n     const currentPendingOutboundMessages = pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.get(options.chatId) || [];\n     const existingPendingOutboundMessage = currentPendingOutboundMessages.find(\n         (pendingOutboundMessage) => pendingOutboundMessage.clientMessageId === options.clientMessageId,\n     );\n \n     const content: options.content,\n               attachments: options.attachments,\n               replyingTo: options.replyingTo,\n               createdAt: (options.createdAt || new Date().toISOString()) currentPendingOutboundMessages.map((pendingOutboundMessage) =>\n               pendingOutboundMessage.clientMessageId === options.clientMessageId\n                   ? nextPendingOutboundMessage\n nextPendingOutboundMessages);\n     return nextPendingOutboundMessage;\n }\n \n /**\n  * Marks one optimistic outbound message as failed while keeping the bubble visible.\n  *\n  * @param options - pendingOutboundMessagesByChatId.get(options.chatId);\n     if (!currentPendingOutboundMessages || currentPendingOutboundMessages.length === 0) {\n         return;\n     }\n \n     const };\n     });\n \n     setPendingOutboundMessagesForChat(options.chatId, nextPendingOutboundMessages);\n }\n that still need to stay rendered locally.\n97.  */\n98. export function reconcilePendingOutboundMessagesWithCanonicalMessages(options: {\n99.     canonicalMessages: ReadonlyArray<ChatMessage>;\n100.     pendingOutboundMessages: ReadonlyArray<PendingOutboundMessageRecord>;\n101. }): Array<PendingOutboundMessageRecord> {\n102.     return options.pendingOutboundMessages.filter((pendingOutboundMessage) => {\n103.         return !options.canonicalMessages.some((canonicalMessage) =>\n104.             isCanonicalMessageConfirmationForPendingOutboundMessage(canonicalMessage, pendingOutboundMessage),\n105.         );\n106.     });\n107. }\n108. \n109. /**\n110.  * Converts one optimistic outbound record into the shared `ChatMessage` shape\n111.  * consumed by the chat UI.\n112.  *\n113.  * @param pendingOutboundMessage - Client-owned optimistic outbound message.\n114.  * @returns Chat bubble rendered alongside canonical transcript messages.\n115.  */\n116. function createOptimisticUserChatMessageFromPendingOutboundMessage(\n117.     pendingOutboundMessage: PendingOutboundMessageRecord,\n118. ): ChatMessage {\n119.     return {\n120.         id: pendingOutboundMessage.tempId,\n121.         sender: 'USER',\n122.         content: pendingOutboundMessage.content,\n123.         attachments: pendingOutboundMessage.attachments,\n124.         replyingTo: pendingOutboundMessage.replyingTo,\n125.         createdAt: pendingOutboundMessage.createdAt,\n126.         isComplete: true,\n127.         lifecycleState: pendingOutboundMessage.status === 'sending' ? 'queued' : 'failed',\n128.         lifecycleError: pendingOutboundMessage.status === 'failed' ? pendingOutboundMessage.errorMessage : undefined,\n129.         clientMessageId: pendingOutboundMessage.clientMessageId,\n130.     };\n131. }\n132. \n133. /**\n134.  * Returns true when one canonical user message confirms an optimistic outbound\n135.  * message already rendered in the browser.\n136.  *\n137.  * The primary reconciliation path uses `clientMessageId`. A bounded fallback\n138.  * compares sender, content, attachments, and timestamp proximity for older or\n139.  * partial transcripts that do not expose the idempotency key yet.\n140.  *\n141.  * @param canonicalMessage - Server-owned canonical transcript message.\n142.  * @param pendingOutboundMessage - Browser-owned optimistic outbound message.\n143.  * @returns `true` when the optimistic bubble should be removed.\n144.  */\n145. function isCanonicalMessageConfirmationForPendingOutboundMessage(\n146.     canonicalMessage: ChatMessage,\n147.     pendingOutboundMessage: PendingOutboundMessageRecord,\n148. ): boolean {\n149.     if (canonicalMessage.sender !== 'USER') {\n150.         return false;\n151.     }\n152. \n153.     if (\n154.         canonicalMessage.clientMessageId &&\n155.         canonicalMessage.clientMessageId === pendingOutboundMessage.clientMessageId\n156.     ) {\n157.         return true;\n158.     }\n159. \n160.     if (canonicalMessage.content !== pendingOutboundMessage.content) {\n161.         return false;\n162.     }\n163. \n164.     if (!areMessageAttachmentsEquivalent(canonicalMessage.attachments, pendingOutboundMessage.attachments)) {\n165.         return false;\n166.     }\n167. \n168.     if (serializeReplyingToSignature(canonicalMessage.replyingTo) !== serializeReplyingToSignature(pendingOutboundMessage.replyingTo)) {\n169.         return false;\n170.     }\n171. \n172.     const canonicalTimestamp = resolveTimestampMilliseconds(canonicalMessage.createdAt);\n173.     const optimisticTimestamp = resolveTimestampMilliseconds(pendingOutboundMessage.createdAt);\n174. \n175.     if (canonicalTimestamp === null || optimisticTimestamp === null) {\n176.         return false;\n177.     }\n178. \n179.     return (\n180.         Math.abs(canonicalTimestamp - optimisticTimestamp) <= PENDING_OUTBOUND_MESSAGE_RECONCILIATION_WINDOW_MS\n181.     );\n182. }\n183. \n184. /**\n185.  * Returns a stable millisecond timestamp for best-effort optimistic fallback\n186.  * reconciliation.\n187.  *\n188.  * @param timestamp - ISO timestamp to parse.\n189.  * @returns Parsed milliseconds or `null` when invalid.\n190.  */\n191. function resolveTimestampMilliseconds(timestamp: string | undefined): number | null {\n192.     if (!timestamp) {\n193.         return null;\n194.     }\n195. \n196.     const parsedTimestamp = new Date(timestamp).getTime();\n197.     return Number.isFinite(parsedTimestamp) ? parsedTimestamp : null;\n198. }\n199. \n200. /**\n201.  * Returns true when canonical and optimistic attachments are equivalent for\n202.  * fallback reconciliation purposes.\n203.  *\n204.  * @param left - Canonical attachments.\n205.  * @param right - Optimistic attachments.\n206.  * @returns `true` when attachments are absent on both sides or serialize equally.\n207.  */\n208. function areMessageAttachmentsEquivalent(\n209.     left: ChatMessage['attachments'],\n210.     right: ChatMessage['attachments'],\n211. ): boolean {\n212.     return serializeAttachmentsForReconciliation(left) === serializeAttachmentsForReconciliation(right);\n213. }\n214. \n215. /**\n216.  * Serializes attachments into a deterministic string for shallow optimistic\n217.  * reconciliation.\n218.  *\n219.  * @param attachments - Attachments to serialize.\n220.  * @returns Stable serialized representation.\n221.  */\n222. function serializeAttachmentsForReconciliation(attachments: ChatMessage['attachments']): string {\n223.     if (!attachments || attachments.length === 0) {\n224.         return '';\n225.     }\n226. \n227.     return JSON.stringify(attachments);\n228. }\n229.
